sections.paragraph:
ἀγαθοεργέω (Pythag., Ep. 10 p. 607; contr. ἀγαθουργέω Ac 14:17; except for use in patristic lit., the verb is quite rare, but ἀγαθουργός and ἀγαθουργίη go back to Hdt. [B-D-F §119, 1]) to do that which benefits others, do good, confer benefits: of the rich 1 Ti 6:18; of God Ac 14:17.—TW.

Pythag Pythag = letters attributed to the phil. Pythagoras (VI–V b.c.); s. Ep.; date uncertain—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

contr. contr. = contracted, -action

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 2
